zh.Beckington, Somerset, England: 英國薩默塞特貝金頓 煙雨 斯坦德維其可
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret St Maur於大約1470年出生在英國戴文時，她的父親John St Maur17歲，母親Elizabeth Choke22歲。她和William Bamfield至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1491年在英國戴文波爾莫享年22歲。

zh-hans.Beckington, Somerset, England: 英国薩默塞特貝金頓 煙雨 斯坦德維其可
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret St Maur于大约1470年出生在英国戴文时，她的父亲John St Maur17岁，母亲Elizabeth Choke22岁。她和William Bamfield至少有2个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于1491年在英国戴文波爾莫享年22岁。

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of the numerous places in France so called from the dedication of their churches to St. Jean (  see  John  ).
2  Americanized form of French   St  .   Jean  .

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Jean
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Francois, Marcel, Yves, Andre, Jacques, Georges, Michel, Philippe, Serge, Yvon, Cecile.

French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), Mauritian, and English (of French Huguenot origin): from the male personal name Jean, French form of John . This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Joseph and Pierre ). It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in the Dominican Republic, Saint Lucia, Saint Martin, and Bahamas).
English: habitational name for someone from Genoa in Italy; compare   Janeway  .

nameMeanings.name: Maur
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ernesto, Ramon, Adela, Carlos, Eladio, Emilia, Estrella, Horacio, Jorge, Jose, Juan, Mario.  Italian Alessandra, Angelo, Cecilio, Cosimo, Gianfranco, Lorenzo, Mauro.
Catalan: from the personal name Mauri, Latin Maurus (see Mauro ).
Catalan and French (Occitan): probably from the personal name Malric (ancient Germanic Almaricus).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret St Maur was born about 1470, in Devon, England, her father, John St Maur, was 17 and her mother, Elizabeth Choke, was 22. She had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with William Bamfield. She died in 1491, in Poltimore, Devon, England, at the age of 22.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret St Maur est née environ 1470, à Devon, Angleterre, son père, John St Maur, avait 17 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Choke, avait 22 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec William Bamfield. Elle est décédée en 1491, à Poltimore, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 22 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret St Maur um 1470 in Devon, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John St Maur 17 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Choke 22. Sie hatte mit William Bamfield mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1491 im Alter von 22 Jahren in Poltimore, Devon, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret St Maur è nata il circa 1470 nel Devon, England, sua padre, John St Maur, aveva 17 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Choke, aveva 22 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con William Bamfield. Morì nel 1491, nel Poltimore, Devon, England, all'età di 22 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret St Maurが約1470年にイギリスデボンで生まれた時，父John St Maurは17歳，母Elizabeth Chokeは22歳であった。彼女はWilliam Bamfieldとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1491年にイギリスデボンポルティモーで22歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret St Maur(이)가 대략 1470년 영국 데폰에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John St Maur(은)는 17세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Choke(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 William Bamfield(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1491년 영국 데폰 포팀어에서 22세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.Beckington, Somerset, England: Beckington with Standerwick, Bro an Hañv, Английн

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret St Maur nasceu aproximadamente 1470, em Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, John St Maur, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Choke, tinha 22 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com William Bamfield. Ela faleceu em 1491, em Poltimore, Devon, Inglaterra, com 22 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret St Maur родилась около 1470 г. в местности девон, Англия, её отцу по имени John St Maur было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Choke, 22. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от William Bamfield. Она умерла в 1491 г. в местности полтимор, девон, Англия в возрасте 22 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret St Maur nació aproximadamente 1470, en Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, John St Maur, tenía 17 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Choke, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con William Bamfield. Murió en 1491, en Poltimore, Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 22 años.
